two-sum.pattern: A hash map of already-seen values turns the pair search into O(1) complement lookups — trading O(n) space for O(n) time.
hints: For each number you visit, what single other value would complete the pair?
You need to ask "have I already seen target minus this number?" in constant time. Which data structure answers membership questions in O(1)?
Walk the array once with a dict mapping value → index. Before storing nums[i], check whether target - nums[i] is already a key; if it is, you have both indices.

tests.description: all-negative values and a negative target
the same element may not be used twice

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    nums = [rng.randrange(-10**9, 10**9) for _ in range(size - 2)]
    a = rng.randrange(-10**8, 10**8)
    b = rng.randrange(-10**8, 10**8)
    nums.append(a)
    nums.append(b)
    return [nums, a + b]
stress.note: should complete well under the run timeout
